source: Curated brother pairs, trios, and groups who played in MLB.
note: From approved themed batch 'Brothers in Baseball' (2026-04-20). Each entry links to authoritative sources for verification.

entries.title: Joe, Dom, and Vince DiMaggio
entries.headline: Three brothers in MLB outfields in the 1940s.

members.name: Joe DiMaggio
members.note: Yankees CF 1936-51. HOF 1955. 3 MVPs, 56-game streak, 9 World Series titles.

members.name: Dom DiMaggio
members.note: Red Sox CF 1940-53. 7-time All-Star. Missed 3 seasons to WWII Navy service.

members.name: Vince DiMaggio
members.note: Five NL teams 1937-46. Two-time All-Star.

entries.title: Felipe, Matty, and Jesús Alou
entries.headline: On September 10 and 15, 1963, all three Alou brothers played in the same Giants outfield simultaneously — the only time three brothers have done so in MLB history.

members.name: Felipe Alou
members.note: 3-time All-Star. Later a 14-year MLB manager (Expos, Giants).

members.name: Matty Alou
members.note: 1966 NL batting champion (.342).

members.name: Jesús Alou
members.note: 15-year MLB outfielder.

links.label: SABR — Alou brothers in same outfield

entries.title: Paul and Lloyd Waner
entries.headline: Both Hall of Famers. Both Pittsburgh Pirates. Roughly 5,600 combined career hits — among the most by any brother pair in MLB history.

members.name: Paul "Big Poison" Waner
members.note: 1927 NL MVP. 3,152 career hits. HOF 1952.

members.name: Lloyd "Little Poison" Waner
members.note: 2,459 career hits. HOF 1967 (Veterans Committee).

entries.title: Dizzy and Paul "Daffy" Dean
entries.headline: 49 combined wins for the 1934 Gas House Gang Cardinals (Dizzy 30-7, Paul 19-11). Each brother won two games in the 1934 World Series.

members.name: Dizzy Dean
members.note: 1934 NL MVP. Last NL pitcher to win 30 games in a season. HOF 1953.

members.name: Paul Dean
members.note: 19-11 as a 21-year-old rookie in 1934. Arm injuries cut his career short.

entries.title: The five Delahanty brothers
entries.headline: Ed, Frank, Jim, Joe, and Tom Delahanty — the only five-brother combination to appear in MLB. Ed's career .346 batting average is still among the highest ever recorded.

members.name: Ed Delahanty
members.note: HOF 1945. Career .346 BA — 5th all-time. Died mysteriously at Niagara Falls in 1903.

members.name: Frank Delahanty
members.note: Six MLB seasons 1905-1915.

members.name: Jim Delahanty
members.note: 13 MLB seasons 1901-1915.

members.name: Joe Delahanty
members.note: Three seasons with the Cardinals 1907-1909.

members.name: Tom Delahanty
members.note: Brief 1890s MLB appearances.

entries.title: Phil and Joe Niekro
entries.headline: 539 combined career wins — the most by any brother pair in MLB history. Both knuckleball specialists.

members.name: Phil Niekro
members.note: 318 career wins. 5-time All-Star. HOF 1997.

members.name: Joe Niekro
members.note: 221 career wins. 1979 NL co-wins leader (21). Longtime Astros ace.

entries.title: Gaylord and Jim Perry
entries.headline: The only brothers to both win a Cy Young Award. Gaylord remains one of very few pitchers to win the award in both leagues.

members.name: Gaylord Perry
members.note: 1972 AL Cy Young (Cleveland), 1978 NL Cy Young (San Diego). 314 career wins. HOF 1991.

members.name: Jim Perry
members.note: 1970 AL Cy Young (Minnesota). 215 career wins. Three-time All-Star.

entries.title: Bengie, José, and Yadier Molina
entries.headline: Three catching brothers, all World Series champions.

members.name: Bengie Molina
members.note: 2002 WS champion (Angels). Two-time Gold Glove.

members.name: José Molina
members.note: WS rings with Yankees (2009) and earlier. Renowned pitch framer.

members.name: Yadier Molina
members.note: Cardinals 2004-2022. 2006 and 2011 WS champion. Nine Gold Gloves.

entries.title: Ken and Clete Boyer
entries.headline: Opposing third basemen in the 1964 World Series — Cards' Ken (that year's NL MVP) and Yankees' Clete. Both homered in Game 7; the Cardinals won the series.

members.name: Ken Boyer
members.note: 1964 NL MVP. 7-time All-Star. Retired #14 by the Cardinals.

members.name: Clete Boyer
members.note: Gold Glove 3B for the Yankees. Homered off teammate Ken in 1964 WS Game 7.

entries.title: B.J. (Melvin) and Justin Upton
entries.headline: Both first-round draft picks. Played together in the Atlanta Braves outfield in 2013.

members.name: B.J. (Melvin) Upton
members.note: 2002 #2 overall draft pick. Two-time All-Star CF.

members.name: Justin Upton
members.note: 2005 #1 overall draft pick. Four-time All-Star. 324+ career HRs.
